changes: Added 5 new events across Iran/UAE/Bahrain/Saudi: IRGC strikes on EGA Al Taweelah (UAE, 6 injured) and Alba (Bahrain, 2 injured) aluminium plants Mar 29; Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure threat endangering Saudi Yanbu Red Sea workaround; 31st MEU (3,500 troops, USS Tripoli, F-35Bs) arrived for potential Kharg Island seizure; IRGC tolling at $2M/vessel in yuan with COSCO vessels turned back despite China friendly status; Thailand/Malaysia secured bilateral Hormuz transit deals. Added 2 new shutdown entries (sd-022 EGA Al Taweelah, sd-021 Alba 2nd strike). Updated Saudi Yanbu to 4.4M bpd (40+ VLCCs queued), Dubai physical at $126/bbl. Added Rystad $25B infrastructure repair estimate. Updated energy-news-data.json with 10 key country-commodity entries. No new FM declarations found.
newEvents: IRGC struck EGA Al Taweelah aluminium (UAE) — 6 injured, significant damage (Mar 29)
IRGC struck Alba smelter (Bahrain) — 2 injured, third attack on Bahrain (Mar 29)
Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure 'among our options' — threatens Saudi Yanbu Red Sea exports (Mar 29)
3,500 US troops (31st MEU/USS Tripoli/F-35Bs) arrived for potential Kharg Island raids (Mar 29)
IRGC tolls at $2M/vessel in Chinese yuan; COSCO ships turned back; Thailand/Malaysia granted access (Mar 27-28)
Saudi Yanbu exports at 4.4M bpd with 40+ VLCCs queued (Mar 28-29)
Dubai physical crude at $126/bbl — 76% above pre-war (Mar 28)
Rystad estimates $25B Gulf infrastructure repair bill, >12M boe/d offline (Mar 26)
India's Operation Urja Suraksha: naval escort of LPG/crude tankers through Hormuz (Mar 26-28)
Sinopec cutting refinery runs 10%; China banned fuel exports Mar 11
55 VLCCs in region (18 above pre-war baseline); 14 staging at Khor Fakkan STS (Kpler)
noNewFmReason: No new FM declarations found in last 48 hours. All 24 existing FMs remain active. Crisis has shifted from FM/commercial phase to military escalation phase — IRGC expanding strikes to Gulf state industrial targets (aluminium plants), Houthis threatening Bab al-Mandeb closure, US 31st MEU positioned for potential ground operations including Kharg Island seizure.

keyRisks: DUAL CHOKEPOINT: Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure threat + Hormuz = near-total ME export blockade. Goldman warns $135 Brent.
GROUND OPS: 31st MEU positioned for Kharg Island seizure — Trump April 6 deadline.
INDUSTRIAL TARGETING: IRGC expanding strikes to Gulf state civilian/industrial infrastructure (aluminium plants).
IRGC INCONSISTENCY: Chinese vessels turned back despite friendly status — undermines trust in tolled passage system.
YANBU BOTTLENECK: 40+ VLCCs queued at Yanbu; port capacity ~4.5M bpd vs pipeline delivering 7M bpd.
